rationale: In the citing paper, Berger and Schwartz (2011) appears as a single supporting citation in a grouped literature sentence about impression-management motives, specifically that people share WOM about interesting products. The paper does not engage this source in detail or use its immediate-versus-ongoing WOM framework, data, or methods in the new model or tests. The citing paper's core theorizing is built around reciprocity and adoption risk in free versus paid settings, so this citation functions as background context rather than a direct building block.
